        "severity_justification": "Incorrect sorting direction would cause wrong blob limits to be applied, potentially accepting blocks with too many or too few blobs. This is a consensus-critical implementation detail.",

        "bug_bounty_notes": "Requires PoC demonstrating wrong sort direction and resulting incorrect blob limit for a specific epoch."
      },

    },
    {
      "property_id": "PROP-0060",

        "id": "PROP-0060",
        "text": "The hashing scheme in compute_fork_digest for blob parameters MUST compute: h = hash(uint_to_bytes(blob_parameters.epoch) + uint_to_bytes(blob_parameters.max_blobs_per_block)). The uint_to_bytes encoding, concatenation order, and hash function MUST be identical across all client implementations.",

        "source_assumption_id": "asm-010",
        "source_invariant_id": null,
        "source_threat_id": "stride-008",
        "covers": {
          "primary_element": "SG-003",
          "nodes": [
            "actor-consensus-layer"
          ],
          "edges": [

          ],
          "is_boundary_edge": true

            "Internal"
          ],
          "attacker_controlled": false,
          "validation_layers": [],
          "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope",
          "notes": "Any deviation in byte encoding (e.g., uint64 vs uint256, big-endian vs little-endian) or concatenation order (epoch+max_blobs vs max_blobs+epoch) would produce different hashes, causing network partitions."
        },
        "exploitability": "internal-bug",
        "severity": "CRITICAL",
        "severity_justification": "Encoding disagreements would cause network partitions. Even a single byte difference in the hash input would produce a completely different digest, isolating non-conforming clients from the network.",
        "bug_bounty_eligible": true,
        "bug_bounty_notes": "Requires cross-client testing showing different hash outputs for the same blob parameters due to encoding differences."
      },
      "source_file": "outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W1B10_1770297331.json"
    },
    {
      "property_id": "PROP-0001",
      "property": {
        "id": "PROP-0001",
        "text": "The fork trigger MUST only fire at epoch boundaries, i.e., when state.slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0. For all slots where slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH != 0, the fork trigger MUST NOT initiate an upgrade.",

        "source_threat_id": null,
        "covers": {
          "primary_element": "fork-SG-001",

      },

    },
    {

        "text": "The fork trigger MUST fire exactly once at FULU_FORK_EPOCH. For all epochs e != FULU_FORK_EPOCH, the fork trigger MUST NOT initiate an upgrade. After the fork has triggered, no subsequent call to the fork trigger may re-trigger the upgrade.",

      "property_id": "PROP-0003",

      "risk_category": "Denial of Service",

      "title": "Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does get_blob_parameters use descending (reverse) epoch sort for BLOB_SCHEDULE lookup?",
      "severity": "High",
      "mindset": "Boundary Guard",
      "is_boundary_check": true,
      "reachability": {
        "classification": "internal-only",

        "attacker_controlled": false,
        "validation_layers": [],
        "bug_bounty_scope": "in-scope",
        "notes": "Sorting direction is critical for correct schedule lookup. Descending sort finds the most recent applicable entry. Ascending sort would find the earliest entry, returning outdated parameters."
      },
      "test_procedure": "1. Create a BLOB_SCHEDULE with multiple entries at different epochs (e.g., epoch 10, 20, 30 with different max_blobs). 2. Query get_blob_parameters for epoch 25. 3. Verify it returns parameters from epoch 20 (most recent applicable), NOT epoch 10 (earliest). 4. Test edge cases: exact epoch match, epoch before all entries, epoch after all entries. 5. Compare results across all client implementations. 6. Verify sorting is stable and deterministic.",

      "risk_category": "Tampering",

      "is_boundary_check": true,

        "notes": "Any deviation in byte encoding (e.g., uint64 vs uint256, big-endian vs little-endian) or concatenation order (epoch+max_blobs vs max_blobs+epoch) would produce different hashes, causing network partitions."
      },
      "test_procedure": "1. Create test vectors with known blob_parameters (epoch, max_blobs_per_block). 2. Compute fork_digest using uint_to_bytes(epoch) + uint_to_bytes(max_blobs_per_block). 3. Verify exact byte representation: check endianness (little-endian per SSZ), byte width (uint64), zero-padding. 4. Compare hash outputs across all client implementations (Lighthouse, Prysm, Teku, Nimbus, Lodestar). 5. Test edge cases: epoch=0, max_blobs=0, maximum values. 6. Verify concatenation order is epoch-first in all implementations.",

    {

Enumerate all state transition functions and verify none can modify fork.current_version. 4. Test reorg scenarios: can a chain reorg crossing the fork boundary cause oscillation? 5. Verify state serialization/deserialization preserves fork version. 6. Check for any code paths that could write to fork.current_version after upgrade.",
